bostonFatalities.fatalitySource: Boston Vision Zero / MassDOT crash data

waymoSafety.waymoRideshareShareSource: Waymo reached ~22% of the San Francisco rideshare market by end of 2024

rideHailVMTShare.note: Ride-hail share of city-core VMT. The 1.76% effective VMT share is derived from Boston ride-hail VMT (~8%) × observed Waymo rideshare penetration in SF (~22%).

waymo.source: Kusano et al., Traffic Injury Prevention (2025), 56.7M miles

rideHailSafetyComparison.caveat: Methodologies differ across these sources. Waymo data uses police-reported incidents matched to location baselines; Uber/Lyft figures are self-reported. Direct comparison should be interpreted with caution.

studies.name: Kusano et al. (2024)
studies.finding: 85% fewer any-injury-reported crashes over 7.1M autonomous miles

studies.name: Kusano et al. (2025)
studies.finding: 85% fewer suspected serious injury+ crashes, 79% fewer any-injury-reported crashes over 56.7M miles

studies.name: Swiss Re / Waymo (2024)
studies.finding: Waymo vehicles had zero bodily injury claims vs. human baseline

permitTimeline.note: AV testing with safety drivers since 2017 — still no legislation permitting driverless operation

timeline.detail: Creates AV testing framework; MassDOT to oversee testing program

timeline.event: nuTonomy & Optimus Ride begin testing in Boston
timeline.detail: First AV companies approved under EO 572 — safety drivers required
Revised process, but no path to driverless commercial operation

timeline.event: Driscoll & Cahill file AV legalization bills
timeline.detail: First legislation to permit fully autonomous vehicles on MA roads
Proposes requiring human safety operators in all AV ride-hail vehicles
Manual-drive testing to validate 6th-gen system for local conditions

timeline.event: Still no legislation permitting driverless operation
timeline.detail: Massachusetts remains without a legal framework for autonomous ride-hail

sources.description: City of Boston traffic safety initiative and crash data

sources.description: Peer-reviewed study: 85% fewer any-injury-reported crashes in 7.1M Waymo miles

sources.description: Zero bodily injury claims for Waymo fleet vs. human baseline

sources.name: MA Executive Order 572 (2016)
sources.description: Governor Baker's executive order creating the AV testing framework in Massachusetts

sources.name: Fehr & Peers Ride-Hail VMT Study (2019)
sources.description: Ride-hail accounts for 1–13% of city-core VMT across major US cities (Boston: ~8%, SF: 12.8%)

sources.name: Kusano et al. (2025) — 56.7M Miles
sources.description: Updated peer-reviewed study: 85% fewer suspected serious injury+ crashes over 56.7M Waymo miles

sources.name: YipitData / App Economy Insights — Waymo SF Market Share
sources.description: Waymo reached ~22% of the San Francisco rideshare market by end of 2024 (YipitData via a16z)
